zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Clare於1293年10月12日出生在英國英格蘭肯特時，她的父親Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester50歲，母親Countess Joan of Acre21歲。她於1307年11月1日與Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall在英國賀特福爾貝可哈姆斯泰德結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1342年4月9日在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾布享年48歲，埋葬在英國肯特托布利其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Clare于1293年10月12日出生在英国英格兰肯特时，她的父亲Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester50岁，母亲Countess Joan of Acre21岁。她于1307年11月1日与Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall在英国賀特福爾貝可哈姆斯泰德结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1342年4月9日在英国英格兰斯塔福爾布享年48岁，埋葬在英国肯特托布利其。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Clare was born on 12 October 1293, in Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester, was 50 and her mother, Countess Joan of Acre, was 21. She married Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall on 1 November 1307, in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 9 April 1342, in Chebsey, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 48, and was buried in Tonbridge, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Clare est née le 12 octobre 1293, à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Countess Joan of Acre, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall le 1 novembre 1307, à Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 9 avril 1342, à Chebsey, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterrée à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre.

Oktober 1293 in Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester 50 und ihre Mutter Countess Joan of Acre 21. Sie hat am 1. November 1307 in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, England Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 9. April 1342 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Chebsey, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Tonbridge, Kent, England begraben.

ko.Westminster Abbey, London, England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Clare(이)가 1293년 10월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Countess Joan of Acre(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1307년 11월 1일 영국 허트퍼드실르 버카스테드에서 Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1342년 4월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 체브시에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 토브리지에 묻혔다.

ru.Westminster Abbey, London, England: вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Clare родилась 12 октября 1293 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Gilbert de Clare 7th Earl of Gloucester было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Countess Joan of Acre, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall 1 ноября 1307 г. в местности бирхамстед, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла 9 апреля 1342 г. в местности шебси, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 48 лет и была похоронена в местности тонбридж, кэнт, Англия.

Se casó con Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall el 1 de noviembre de 1307, en Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 9 de abril de 1342, en Chebsey, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultada en Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra.
